title: Back-Rank Bedrock
setup: Black's king is hiding behind **f7**, **g7** and **h7** — pawns that have become a wall.
hint: The 8th rank is wide open and Black's pawns have not made an escape window on {{h6}} or {{g6}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the textbook **back-rank mate**. The rook slides into the corner and the king cannot escape because his own pawns block every flight square.

Play {{Ra8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{f7}}, {{g7}}, {{h7}} — all blocked by Black's own pawns
- {{f8}} and {{h8}} — both swept by the rook on a8
- No piece is left to interpose between a8 and g8

> **Takeaway:** The price of safety is one tempo — a single pawn move (h6, g6, or f6) opens an air hole and prevents this disaster. Train your eye to spot this skeleton in every game.
